title: celtis zippelii
intro: Celtis zippelii, also known as the Zippel's hackberry or Taiwanese hackberry, is a plant species native to Taiwan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an antioxidant. In traditional herbal medicine, Celtis zippelii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it may also be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
